title: OWASP Top 10 - Broken Access Control
description: Access control enforces policy so users cannot act outside their intended permissions. Failures lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction.
anti_patterns: Bypassing access checks via URL manipulation

title: OWASP Top 10 - Cryptographic Failures
description: Failures related to cryptography that lead to sensitive data exposure. Includes weak algorithms, poor key management, and missing encryption.
best_practices: Encrypt data at rest and in transit

title: OWASP Top 10 - Injection
description: Injection flaws occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query. Includes SQL, NoSQL, OS, and LDAP injection.

title: OWASP Top 10 - Insecure Design
description: Focuses on risks related to design and architectural flaws. Calls for threat modeling, secure design patterns, and reference architectures.

title: OWASP Top 10 - Security Misconfiguration
description: Missing or incorrect security configurations across the application stack. Includes default credentials, unnecessary features, and missing security headers.

title: OWASP Top 10 - Vulnerable Components
description: Using components with known vulnerabilities. Organizations must know versions of all components used and monitor for CVEs.

title: OWASP Top 10 - Authentication Failures
description: Confirmation of the user's identity, authentication, and session management. Weaknesses allow attackers to compromise passwords, keys, or session tokens.

title: OWASP API Security - Broken Object Level Authorization
description: APIs expose endpoints that handle object identifiers. Every function accessing a data source using user input should verify authorization.
best_practices: Check authorization for every object access
anti_patterns: Predictable resource IDs without auth checks
Relying solely on client-side ID filtering

title: OWASP API Security - Broken Authentication
description: Authentication mechanisms in APIs are often implemented incorrectly, allowing attackers to compromise authentication tokens or exploit flaws.
best_practices: Use standard authentication protocols (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect)
Use rate limiting on auth endpoints

title: OWASP API Security - Excessive Data Exposure
description: APIs may expose more data than the client needs, relying on the client to filter sensitive data. This is a common API design flaw.
best_practices: Use DTOs to control output shape

title: Zero Trust Architecture
description: Security model that requires strict identity verification for every person and device trying to access resources, regardless of network location.

title: Secrets Management
description: Securely storing, accessing, and managing sensitive data such as API keys, passwords, certificates, and encryption keys.
best_practices: Use dedicated secrets managers (AWS Secrets Manager, Vault)
Never commit secrets to version control
anti_patterns: Secrets in environment variables without encryption

title: Authentication Best Practices
description: Implementing robust authentication mechanisms to verify user identity. Includes password-based auth, OAuth, MFA, and passwordless options.
best_practices: Use OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect for SSO
Implement account lockout after failed attempts

title: Authorization Patterns
description: Controlling what authenticated users can access and do. Includes RBAC, ABAC, and policy-based access control patterns.

title: API Security Best Practices
description: Securing APIs against common attack vectors including injection, authentication bypass, rate limiting bypass, and data exposure.

title: Encryption at Rest and in Transit
description: Protecting data through encryption when stored (at rest) and when being transmitted (in transit) between services and clients.
best_practices: Encrypt sensitive data at rest with AES-256
Use AWS KMS for key management
Enable encryption by default on all storage services

title: Input Validation and Sanitization
description: Ensuring all user input is validated, sanitized, and constrained before processing to prevent injection attacks and data corruption.
best_practices: Validate on both client and server

title: Logging and Monitoring Security
description: Implementing comprehensive logging and monitoring to detect security incidents, support forensics, and meet compliance requirements.
